content.body: We’ve got Aki and ZB on the agenda today.

CRA annex 1: https://expertzebra.com/cra/#d1e38-68-1
email me your questions, **especially** if you think I should answer them at full plenary. The CRA is big and I want to provide a somewhat focused overview for TC39 if I'm going to take up their time
aki.braun@ecma-international.org
content.formatted_body:
CRA annex 1: https://expertzebra.com/cra/#d1e38-68-1
email me your questions, especially if you think I should answer them at full plenary. The CRA is big and I want to provide a somewhat focused overview for TC39 if I'm going to take up their time
aki.braun@ecma-international.org

content.body: So contemplating the specific questions y'all had, here are some meaningful details for your particularly security-minded thought processes to churn on: the regulation intended by the CRA revolves around "reasonably foreseeable use" (i.e. use cases) and taking a risk-based approach. not everyone needs the highest security settings for every purpose. "secure by default" refers to settings that the user can change and also how the user is going to use the product and what risks are relevant for their use. Not everyone needs lockdown mode on their phone.
Are Zb's slides available? I could try to get some early feedback from SpiderMonkey folks if so.

These were drafts with alternatives to include and the conclusion was it needs to be folded into Compartment, so the presentable fragment that could be shared is how ScopeCeiling would be introduced as OuterEnv to Module Environment Record.
I'll put together a short document representing that part without the  parts where I was looking for places to introduce it without a Compartment
That would be great, thank you
